title: Pass 5.G.5: ferrotorch-llama beam_search / spec_decode K+1 forwards per step (no KV-cache)
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-llama audit, PERF_OBVIOUS.

- src/spec_decode.rs:264-290 — K verify forwards per round, each rebuilding prefix = context + draft_tokens[0..j] and recomputing all prior tokens. Quadratic in draft_k per generation step; K=4 default already costs ~5× target work vs. the paper's batched verify.
- src/generation.rs:442-499 (beam_search) — same shape: per step, runs live.len() full-prefix forward passes (forward_from_ids) with no KV-cache. With num_beams=4 this is 4× cost of greedy on a hot path, every step.
- src/gpu.rs:818-854 (build_rope_caches) — Llama 3.3 70B max_position_embeddings = 131_072, head_dim = 128 → 16.7M f64 trig calls on the host at construct time. Visible startup latency.

Architectural blocker: spec_decode.rs:42-53 ModelHandle::forward_ids returns only last-position logits as Vec<f64>; structural reason a batched-verify path is impossible without a trait extension.

Acceptance:
- Extend ModelHandle trait with batched verify (returns per-position logits)
- Wire KV-cache through beam_search live-prefix forwards (PyTorch generate() uses past_key_values)
- f32 cos/sin for build_rope_caches (or vectorized f64); cache once across model instances if config matches.
